publicSurface.rule: unsoccer/index.html is the public static page for the local server and production route until the built client is deployed there.
UI Designer owns public page hierarchy, prototype status copy, catalog continuity, and itch.io presentation fit.
The public route must not expose a directory listing.

rules.rule: Client bundles must not import server-only code.

v0.0.002 keeps Russian player-facing text, HDR-style environment lighting, a visible sun, a 120-second realtime day cycle, an inertial perspective follow camera, and distinct server-authoritative body/left-foot/right-foot contacts.
v0.0.003 keeps weather and hazards server-authoritative; any puddle, slush, or snowbank that changes movement or ball physics must also be visible in the client.
v0.0.007 aligns public client/server version and keeps production transport dependency-free: built-in Node WebSocket JSON plus HTTP polling fallback.
v0.0.008 keeps the version and client weight always visible and starts the residential courtyard visual pass around the snowy field.

v0.0.008 audio uses a server-authored audioEvents ring buffer in ServerState so roster, kick/body, goal, and countdown cues synchronize across WebSocket and HTTP fallback clients by authoritative event id.

v0.0.009 expands the runtime Art Director pass with courtyard models, procedural animated footballer rigs, visible sun/moon/orbit marker, ambient/bounce lighting, and QA-readable art/day-cycle datasets.
v0.0.009 makes WASD team-relative, keeps local controlled movement responsive with a short client prediction lead, and removes artificial pitch bounds from player movement.

v0.0.009 ships the UI Designer runtime pass with redesigned HUD hierarchy, event/status/network panels, toolbar buttons, settings modal tabs, localStorage settings, remappable input, runtime audio/graphics/network/accessibility controls, and QA-readable UI debug datasets.
v0.0.010 is the unified 0010 release build with all current runtime workstreams, a camera-framed sun/moon marker, synchronized version surfaces, and repo-side publication evidence.

v0.0.011 doubles the field/courtyard, starts authoritative time at 06:00, rotates sun/moon by server day time, randomizes weather every 60-120s, adds dawn birds/day traffic audio, moving cars, sprint/stamina/exhaustion/jump, foot/hand/head player hits, bouncier ball physics, thicker goal rebounds, local cloth net ripples, and 10 local Free3D vertex-color soccer-ball GLBs.

v0.0.012 keeps the current scene size, fixes server-driven day cycle under qaTime=0, makes 04:00-20:00 bright daytime and 20:00-04:00 the dark window, hides the old sun-path debug line, prevents clear weather precipitation particles, weights random weather toward clear/dawn, upgrades the goal net to client-only Verlet cloth, shows the active Free3D vertex-color GLB ball on-field while hiding that rack variant, aligns visible goal posts with thicker server colliders, and acceptance-tests post rebounds plus bouncier heading physics.

v0.0.013 replaces the primary primitive player body with a local Free3D Online 6300420 skinned GLB character optimized to 2.09 MB, cloned with SkeletonUtils, animated with per-player AnimationMixer, and keeps procedural primitives only as loading/error fallback.

v0.0.014 replaces the white all-animation character runtime with a different textured Free3D 6299851 split pipeline: rigged_unity.glb, WebP albedo/normal/ORM maps, and separate FBX idle/walk/run/jump clips selected by player state.
Server-confirmed left-foot, hand, head, jump, and body contacts must stay visually distinguishable and expose data-last-action-kind, data-last-action-player, and data-last-action-at for QA.
Server owns physics, score, teams, and room assignment.

Production transport is plain WebSocket JSON under /unsoccer/socket/ with HTTP polling fallback, implemented with built-in Node http/net/crypto primitives; do not require geckos.io, ws, or native node-datachannel on the Qwertystock host.
UNSOCCER_TEST_MODE enables /api/test/* only for isolated local acceptance and must not be exposed in production.

npm run test:unsoccer:acceptance is the deterministic gate for spectator roles, team-relative WASD movement, HTTP fallback join/input/state, pitch-bound freedom, foot/hand/head kicks, jump, sprint/stamina, weather, goal reset, and server audioEvents.
Acceptance derives the expected version from package.json.games.unsoccer.version and must not hard-code a release string.
npm run package:unsoccer rebuilds before creating dist/unsoccer-itch.zip so stale dist output is not published.
Client audio is procedural Web Audio driven by authoritative ServerState audioEvents and weather state, not local input prediction.
Audio QA must inspect data-audio-played-events, data-audio-blocked-events, data-audio-context, and data-audio-unlocked after a real trusted browser gesture.
First 4 clients are players; clients 5-32 are spectators/testers.
Itch package contains static built client only.
Free3D runtime assets require local GLB files and provenance; tokenless LowPoly 1k assets must be selected only from exact public worker inventory paths.json entries.
